question: How does the financial burden experienced by Nario Ctis, a 60-year-old teacher earning €776 monthly, relate to the graffiti message '500 salary 400 EUR rent' shown in Athens?
answer: Nario Ctis reported paying €500 to €600 in rent, aligning with the graffiti's statement of "500 salary 400 EUR rent" that highlights the disproportionate income-to-rent ratio affecting residents.

video: Graffiti on a wall reads 'Airbnb raises the rents' alongside an illustration of buildings.
text: Nario Ctis, a 60-year-old teacher earning €776, reported paying €500 to €600 in rent and stated "clearly they can't survive."
Graffiti in Athens expressed opposition to short-term rentals, with one reading "live the real Greek experience 500 salary 400 EUR rent Airbnb raises the rents."
